# Meridian Treatise Sample

## Circulation of the Twelve Meridians

### Lung Meridian of Hand-Taiyin
Originates in the middle jiao, descends to connect with the large intestine, returns to circle the stomach, ascends across the diaphragm to belong to the lung. From the lung system, it transversely exits the axilla, descends along the medial aspect of the upper arm, traveling anterior to the Heart Meridian of Hand-Shaoyin and Pericardium Meridian of Hand-Jueyin, enters the cubital fossa, follows the medial aspect of the forearm along the radial bone, enters the cunkou (wrist pulse), ascends to the thenar eminence, follows the thenar eminence, and exits at the tip of the thumb. Its branch: from behind the wrist, straightly exits to the medial side of the index finger, ending at its tip.

### Large Intestine Meridian of Hand-Yangming
Originates at the tip of the index finger, follows the radial side of the finger, exits between the first and second metacarpal bones, enters the space between the two tendons, follows the lateral aspect of the forearm, enters the lateral aspect of the elbow, ascends the lateral anterior aspect of the upper arm, reaches the shoulder, exits anterior to the acromion, ascends to the meeting point of the Du Meridian at the highest point of the shoulder, descends into the supraclavicular fossa, connects with the lung, descends across the diaphragm, and belongs to the large intestine. Its branch: from the supraclavicular fossa, ascends the neck, penetrates the cheek, enters the lower teeth, returns to curve around the mouth, crosses the philtrum, from left to right and right to left, ascending to flanking the nostrils.

### Stomach Meridian of Foot-Yangming
Originates at the meeting point of the nose, joins the Bladder Meridian of Foot-Taiyang at the side, descends along the lateral side of the nose, enters the upper teeth, returns to curve around the mouth, circles the lips, descends to meet at the mentolabial groove, then follows the lower margin of the mandible, exits at Daying (ST 5), follows the angle of the mandible, ascends in front of the ear, passes through Tinggong (SI 19), follows the hairline, and reaches the forehead. Its branch: from before Daying (ST 5), descends to Renying (ST 9), follows the throat, enters the supraclavicular fossa, descends across the diaphragm, belongs to the stomach, and connects with the spleen. Its straight branch: from the supraclavicular fossa, descends the medial aspect of the breast, follows the lateral side of the umbilicus, and enters the qijie (气街, energy thoroughfare). Its branch: originates from the stomach orifice, descends along the abdomen, and joins at the qijie. From there, it descends through Biguan (ST 31), reaches Futu (ST 32), descends the knee, follows the lateral aspect of the tibia, descends to the dorsum of the foot, and enters the space between the middle toes. Its branch: separates 3 cun below the knee, descends to the lateral side of the middle toe. Its branch: separates on the dorsum of the foot, enters the space between the first and second toes, ending at its tip.

### Spleen Meridian of Foot-Taiyin
Originates at the tip of the big toe, follows the medial side of the toe along the white skin margin, passes behind the ball of the foot, ascends the anterior aspect of the medial malleolus, ascends the medial aspect of the lower leg, follows the posterior aspect of the tibia, crosses and emerges anterior to the Liver Meridian of Foot-Jueyin, ascends the medial aspect of the thigh, enters the abdomen, belongs to the spleen, connects with the stomach, ascends across the diaphragm, flanks the throat, connects with the root of the tongue, and spreads over the lower surface of the tongue. Its branch: from the stomach, ascends across the diaphragm, and flows into the heart.

### Heart Meridian of Hand-Shaoyin
Originates in the heart, exits to belong to the heart system, descends across the diaphragm, and connects with the small intestine. Its branch: from the heart system, ascends to flank the throat, and connects with the eye system. Its straight branch: from the heart system, ascends to the lung, descends to exit the axilla, follows the medial posterior aspect of the upper arm, traveling posterior to the Lung Meridian of Hand-Taiyin and Pericardium Meridian of Hand-Jueyin, enters the cubital fossa, follows the medial posterior aspect of the forearm, reaches the ulnar side of the wrist, enters the palm, follows the medial side of the little finger, and exits at its tip.

### Small Intestine Meridian of Hand-Taiyang
Originates at the tip of the little finger, follows the lateral side of the hand, ascends the wrist, exits the ulnar styloid process, ascends straight along the posterior aspect of the forearm, exits between the two bones at the medial aspect of the elbow, ascends the lateral posterior aspect of the upper arm, exits the scapular region, circles the scapula, crosses the shoulder, enters the supraclavicular fossa, connects with the heart, follows the esophagus, descends across the diaphragm, reaches the stomach, and belongs to the small intestine. Its branch: from the supraclavicular fossa, ascends the neck, reaches the cheek, goes to the outer canthus, and enters the ear. Its branch: from the cheek, ascends to the inferior margin of the nose, reaches the inner canthus, and obliquely connects to the zygomatic region.

### Bladder Meridian of Foot-Taiyang
Originates at the inner canthus, ascends the forehead, and meets at the vertex. Its branch: from the vertex to the upper angle of the ear. Its straight branch: from the vertex, enters to connect with the brain, returns to exit and descends along the posterior aspect of the neck, follows the medial aspect of the scapulae, flanks the spine, reaches the lumbar region, enters the paravertebral muscles, connects with the kidney, and belongs to the bladder. Its branch: from the lumbar region, descends flanking the spine, penetrates the gluteal region, and enters the popliteal fossa. Its branch: from the medial aspect of the scapulae, descends bilaterally through the scapulae, flanks the spine internally, passes through the hip joint, follows the lateral posterior aspect of the thigh, and joins in the popliteal fossa — from there, it descends through the calf, exits posterior to the lateral malleolus, follows Jinggu (BL 64) to the lateral side of the little toe.

### Kidney Meridian of Foot-Shaoyin
Originates below the little toe, curves to the sole of the foot, emerges below Rangu (KI 2), follows behind the medial malleolus, enters the heel, ascends the medial aspect of the lower leg, exits the medial aspect of the popliteal fossa, ascends the medial posterior aspect of the thigh, penetrates the spine to belong to the kidney, and connects with the bladder. Its straight branch: from the kidney, ascends through the liver and diaphragm, enters the lung, follows the throat, and flanks the root of the tongue. Its branch: from the lung, connects with the heart, and flows into the chest.

### Pericardium Meridian of Hand-Jueyin
Originates in the chest, exits to belong to the pericardium, descends across the diaphragm, and sequentially connects with the triple energizer. Its branch: follows the chest, exits the costal region, descends 3 cun below the axilla, ascends to the axilla, follows the medial aspect of the upper arm, traveling between the Lung Meridian of Hand-Taiyin and Heart Meridian of Hand-Shaoyin, enters the cubital fossa, descends the forearm between the two tendons, enters the palm, follows the middle finger, and exits at its tip. Its branch: separates in the palm, follows the ring finger, and exits at its tip.

### Triple Energizer Meridian of Hand-Shaoyang
Originates at the tip of the ring finger, ascends through the space between the ring and middle fingers, follows the dorsal aspect of the wrist, exits between the two bones of the forearm, ascends through the elbow, follows the lateral aspect of the upper arm, ascends the shoulder, and crosses behind the Gallbladder Meridian of Foot-Shaoyang, enters the supraclavicular fossa, spreads in the chest, disperses to connect with the pericardium, descends across the diaphragm, and pertains to the triple energizer. Its branch: from the chest, ascends the supraclavicular fossa, ascends the neck, connects behind the ear, ascends straight to the upper angle of the ear, bends downward to the cheek, and reaches the mandible. Its branch: from behind the ear, enters the ear, exits in front of the ear, passes in front of Tinggong (SI 19), crosses the cheek, and reaches the outer canthus.

### Gallbladder Meridian of Foot-Shaoyang
Originates at the outer canthus, ascends to the corner of the forehead, descends behind the ear, follows the neck, travels anterior to the Triple Energizer Meridian of Hand-Shaoyang, reaches the shoulder, then crosses behind the Triple Energizer Meridian of Hand-Shaoyang, and enters the supraclavicular fossa. Its branch: from behind the ear, enters the ear, exits in front of the ear, and reaches behind the outer canthus. Its branch: separates from the outer canthus, descends to Daying (ST 5), joins the Triple Energizer Meridian of Hand-Shaoyang, reaches the mandible, descends to Jiache (ST 6), descends the neck, and joins the supraclavicular fossa — from there, it descends the chest, penetrates the diaphragm, connects with the liver, belongs to the gallbladder, follows the costal region, exits the qijie, circles the pubic region, and transversely enters the hip joint. Its straight branch: from the supraclavicular fossa, descends the axilla, follows the chest, passes the hypochondrium, and joins in the hip joint. From there, it follows the lateral aspect of the thigh, exits the lateral aspect of the knee, descends the anterior aspect of the fibula, straightly descends to the tip of the fibula, descends anterior to the lateral malleolus, follows the dorsum of the foot, and enters the space between the fourth and fifth toes. Its branch: separates on the dorsum of the foot, enters the space between the first and second toes, follows the medial aspect of the first metatarsal bone, ends at its tip, and then penetrates the toenail to reach the三毛 region.

### Liver Meridian of Foot-Jueyin
Originates at the lateral side of the big toe, ascends along the dorsum of the foot, 1 cun away from the medial malleolus, ascends 8 cun above the medial malleolus, crosses and emerges posterior to the Spleen Meridian of Foot-Taiyin, ascends the medial aspect of the popliteal fossa, follows the medial aspect of the thigh, enters the pubic region, circles the external genitalia, reaches the lower abdomen, flanks the stomach, belongs to the liver, connects with the gallbladder, ascends through the diaphragm, spreads over the costal region, follows the posterior aspect of the throat, enters the nasopharynx, connects with the eye system, ascends to the forehead, and meets the Du Meridian at the vertex. Its branch: from the eye system, descends the cheek, and circles the inner aspect of the lips. Its branch: again separates from the liver, penetrates the diaphragm, and flows into the lung.

## Commonly Used Acupoints

### Hegu (LI 4)
Location: On the dorsum of the hand, between the first and second metacarpal bones, at the midpoint of the second metacarpal bone on the radial side.
Indications: Headache, redness and swelling of the eyes, nosebleed, toothache, lockjaw, facial paralysis, deafness, mumps, sore throat, febrile disease without sweating, excessive sweating, abdominal pain, constipation, amenorrhea, difficult labor.

### Zusanli (ST 36)
Location: On the anterolateral side of the lower leg, 3 cun below Dubi (ST 35), one finger-width (middle finger) lateral to the anterior crest of the tibia.
Indications: Stomachache, vomiting, dysphagia, abdominal distension, diarrhea, dysentery, constipation, breast abscess, intestinal abscess, lower limb pain, edema, mania, beriberi, consumptive disease with emaciation.

### Sanyinjiao (SP 6)
Location: On the medial side of the lower leg, 3 cun above the tip of the medial malleolus, posterior to the medial crest of the tibia.
Indications: Borborygmus, abdominal distension, diarrhea, irregular menstruation, leukorrhea, uterine prolapse, infertility, difficult labor, spermatorrhea, impotence, enuresis, hernia, insomnia, lower limb weakness, beriberi.

### Guanyuan (CV 4)
Location: On the lower abdomen, on the anterior midline, 3 cun below the umbilicus.
Indications: Collapse syndrome, consumptive cold and exhaustion, emaciation and weakness, lower abdominal pain, cholera with vomiting and diarrhea, dysentery, rectal prolapse, hernia, blood in the stool, blood in the urine, difficult urination, frequent urination, urinary retention, spermatorrhea, white turbidity, impotence, premature ejaculation, irregular menstruation, amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea, leukorrhea, uterine prolapse, uterine bleeding, vaginal itching, lochiorrhea, retained placenta, diabetes, dizziness.
